records.ety: [L. abhorrere; ab + horrere to bristle, shiver, shudder: cf. F. abhorrer. See Horrid.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Abhorred (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Abhorring.]

defs: To shrink back with shuddering from; to regard with horror or detestation; to feel excessive repugnance toward; to detest to extremity; to loathe.
quotes: Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good.

defs: To fill with horror or disgust.
quotes: It doth abhor me now I speak the word.

defs: To protest against; to reject solemnly.
quotes: I utterly abhor, yea, from my soul Refuse you for my judge.

defs: To shrink back with horror, disgust, or dislike; to be contrary or averse; -- with
quotes: Which is utterly abhorring from the end of all law.
